<Meta title="Overview/Getting Started" />

# Getting Started

## Designers

Designers can use the [Figma kit for Lift](https://www.figma.com/design/HA7RCY6fiLpetLwLPGQdHI/Lift-Design-System?node-id=4174-43977)
to use Lift's components in their designs.

## Developers

### Installation

_Coming soon: a setup guide for how to add Lift to your project_

### Usage

Most Lift components are built as [web components](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Web_components),
so that they can be used on any page, using any framework. They act like native HTML tags, and use an HTML syntax.
For instance, to add a Button component to your page, you'd just add this to your page as HTML:

```html
<atp-button label="My Button"></atp-button>
```

A few Lift features (notably typographic styling, tables, and description lists) are done using CSS classes instead.
See the [Typography](?path=/docs/foundations-typography--docs),
[Table](?path=/docs/components-table--table), and
[Description List](?path=/docs/components-descriptionlist--descriptionlist)
pages for more.
